name: Promote latest source and restart from the UI

rounds.prompt: Implement a UI-first dogfood source-promotion workflow for a running Refine checkout. Add an explicit source/dogfood update channel distinct from the existing published-release updater. From the System UI, show the running checkout path, current commit, configured remote/branch, fetched available commit, and controls to check for source updates and promote the latest source. Promotion must use shared backend services with thin UI/API adapters; require a clean controller checkout, fast-forward-only ancestry, an idle/quiescent Refine runtime, and a successful candidate build before activation. Because the daemon must stop and restart, use a restart-safe external handoff/helper that outlives the HTTP request, persists stage-by-stage operation state under the port runtime root, supports UI reconnect/poll/SSE feedback, verifies daemon health after restart, and reports actionable stage failures plus rollback/recovery information. Never reset, merge, overwrite dirty work, or silently stop active Goal work. Keep the existing release-channel updater intact. Add CLI/API parity where appropriate, System UI controls and accessible states, focused unit/web tests for success, dirty/non-fast-forward/active-operation/build/restart failures, and documentation/spec updates. Tests must not perform a real self-update.
